title: My Work - only awful for me?
excerpt: HELP! I’ve never been more frustrated in my life… I’m a project manager for a smaller company so there’s absolutely no way i’m the only one having these issues. When “My Work”'s beta version rolled out I jumped on it bef…

posts.author: kellyzimmerman (Kelly Zimmerman)
posts.cookedHtml:
HELP! I’ve never been more frustrated in my life… I’m a project manager for a smaller company so there’s absolutely no way i’m the only one having these issues. When “My Work”'s beta version rolled out I jumped on it before my team would have the opportunity to so I could get adjusted, as I’m known as the go-to for monday.com support. It has great potential but the bugs are excruciating… I fought back and forth with their customer service team back in July and they promised me that the issues would be resolved before it fully rolled out. I work 50-60 hour weeks so I didn’t have time to argue anymore so I gave them the benefit of the doubt.

It just rolled out for my team and it’s JUST as bad as it was before. Absolutely nothing has changed from the beta version… and here we are again trying to get customer service to do ANYTHING and they give me the run-around. It’s insanely frustrating. When “my work” isn’t functioning properly how am I or anyone on my team supposed to know what items they need to do each day??? I currently can’t do my job and I’ve spent the last 12 hours shifting around my boards to try to get them to work with “my work” including deleting all of my templates because they count towards the item limit…

Suggestions/Issues (that I’ve already given monday.com back in July and again today)

  • As a project manager I plan projects 6-12 months in advance. The thought that we only have 500 assignments is insane. We have 100 assignments per week on average. There should be an option to NOT show the “future items” and “items without a date” and have them not count towards our item limit. And yes, before you ask I DO have it checked off to hide all completed items.
  • The customizations purely aren’t working… multiple boards aren’t showing up under “Boards” (and no they’re not under hidden boards). And those boards that ARE showing up only populate some of the assignments into my task list… there are multiple items missing. How am I supposed to have this as my and my team’s task list if it’s unreliable? Not only that but every time I open a new monday board the “Boards” list under customizations changes so my entire “My Work” shifts around and shows me different assignments…
  • After deleting a lot of my templates and clearing out the “items without a date” so that I could see my actual assignments my “My Week” is now only showing 250 assignments and claiming that it’s at its limit… WHY

HOW on this green earth did this get launched with this many bugs??? Monday.com is 100% unusable for my team and I. The last thing I need added to my plate is to transition to a new project management software. So if anyone’s gotten this far PLEASE for the love of all things good… please share some tips

posts.author: kellyzimmerman (Kelly Zimmerman)
posts.cookedHtml:
In the case that anyone stumbles upon this post in their own frustration with “My Work” I seem to have had a breakthrough with a solution.

This definitely isn’t the most advanced solution, although, I prefer to keep things on the simple side of things!

I went through all of my setup project templates and removed everyone assigned to them, that way they’re not showing up under “without a date” - when I use the templates I’ll just have to reassign.

Solution: Create an “Upcoming” status and update the settings so that the Upcoming status sets all assignments with that status as done. Update all upcoming projects/assignments as “Upcoming” so that those assignments aren’t pulling into My Work. Create an automation/trigger/assignment for your coordinator, project manager, etc. for about 1 month out depending on the project, for that person to remove all of the “Upcoming” statuses so that they then populate into everyone’s My Work.

Hope this helps someone else!

posts.author: goldmandw (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: Kelly I am having some of the same frustrations as you. We have due and past due tasks that just don’t show up under my work for some reason.

posts.author: kellyzimmerman (Kelly Zimmerman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Dylan, we have been having the same issues. I created workarounds that don’t end up working because your completed tasks apparently count towards your item limits. I’ve been going back and forth with monday.com support all week trying to figure something out. They even increased our item limit and it’s still not enough.

The only thing that has worked for now is creating a My Week dashboard widget and working off of that list. If they can’t fix this by Friday I’ll be holding demos with Asana and Wrike, which is super disappointing. Hopefully they can figure something out!

posts.author: stefan.b (Stefan Bergmeier)
posts.cookedHtml: I also have these problems with the new »my work« feature. Some of my tasks aren’t showing up. Gladly »my week« is still working: [my-company].monday.com/my_week

posts.author: JasonJ (Jason Carter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep- We are leaving Monday because of this.
How on earth can task management software, specifically designed for tasks, limit you to 750 items??? I mean what the hell, you can’t see any more tasks after that? So what are we supposed to do, remember all the ones that can’t make the list? It renders the software completely useless.
My mind is actually serioulsy blown!

posts.author: stefan.b (Stefan Bergmeier)
posts.cookedHtml: I found the error why some tasks didn’t show up in My Work but on the old My Week. I had to add a new person column in those missing projects and assign me again. So there seems to be a bug in some of the persons column.

posts.author: jimmylavoie (Jimmy Lavoie)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, I’m new to Monday.com and that’s really good to know! I’ve just checked out, and I don’t understand why they did replace this feature with My Work. It looks much more productive with the My Week view!

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing that tip. We’ve been dealing with the My Work / missing pulses bug for more than a year, with dozens of email exchanges to Monday Support. They have never offered a solution, but your suggestion worked.

posts.author: VirtualVines (Roberto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys!

We have an alternative solution to My Work. Our app, MyWeek, allows you to view activities in a calendar view of a week.

Key Features:

  • Centralize all activities in one dynamic weekly view

  • Customize your week to start on any day

  • Hide weekends for streamlined work planning

  • Group activities by project boards for perfect organization

  • Effortlessly drag and resize schedule items

  • Personalize your view with flexible columns (date/timeline, people & status)

  • Create and edit activities directly within the app

  • Navigate instantly to any day or week with our intuitive calendar

Look for “MyWeek” in monday’s marketplace. https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000870/myweek

We’re constantly updating our app with new features. You can write to us and we’ll develop what you need. https://wkf.ms/3RUoye3

posts.author: Moeydc (Jon Moses)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have abandoned mywork all together,

Setup a new board for each employee (or taskee)

Once a status is triggered the job items are sent to that persons board, same if a person is assigned a role on the board - an automation sends it to their board.

Jobs are connected back to their OG source so they can be marked off on both boards, for example the OG board status moves to the next stage, and the job board gets marked as ‘Done” removing the task.

I have a status on the job board that is ‘Send to - Tom’ and it will move items to the other persons board (delegate)

I also have different groups setup on the board So i can allocate what is today, tomorrow etc,

I also have a status to remind me in 1 day/ 1 week/ 1 month etc that removes the job from the group, sends to a holding group and then moves it back after the timeframe.

The above works SO well, I haven’t looked at mywork in 18 months
